question: What are the two primary resources provided in the 'Get started' section for learning Docker, and what is the intended progression between them?
answer: The 'Get started' section provides guides to install Docker and jump into discovering what Docker is, followed by learning foundational concepts and workflows; the intended progression is from installation and basic discovery to understanding core concepts and workflows.
supporting_sentences: If you're new to Docker, this section guides you through the essential resources to get started.
Install Docker and jump into discovering what Docker is.
Learn the foundational concepts and workflows of Docker.

question: How many distinct types of cookies are described in the document, and what is the order of their presentation from most to least restrictive in terms of user control?
answer: Four distinct types of cookies are described; the order from most to least restrictive in terms of user control is: Necessary cookies, Functionality cookies, Analytics cookies, Advertising cookies.
supporting_sentences: These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems.
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation.
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site.
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners.

question: Under what circumstances would blocking 'Functionality cookies' impact the user experience on the Docker documentation site, and what specific services might be affected?
answer: Blocking 'Functionality cookies' would impact the user experience if third-party services or enhanced personalization features are relied upon; some or all of these services may not function properly, such as AI assistants like Gordon or other integrated tools.
supporting_sentences: These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages.
If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
I'm Gordon, your AI assistant for Docker and documentation questions.

question: What is the difference between the purpose of 'Analytics cookies' and 'Advertising cookies' as described in the document?
answer: Analytics cookies are used to measure and improve site performance by tracking visits and traffic sources, while Advertising cookies are used to build a profile of user interests and show relevant ads on other sites.
supporting_sentences: These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site.
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites.

question: If a user opts out of 'Advertising cookies,' what specific outcome does the document state will occur, and how does this differ from opting out of 'Analytics cookies'?
answer: Opting out of 'Advertising cookies' will result in less targeted advertising; opting out of 'Analytics cookies' will prevent the site from knowing when the user has visited and from monitoring its performance.
supporting_sentences: If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.
If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
